spec_match.rationale: Demo invokes the relevant API surface (WebGPU 'core-features-and-limits') and surfaces the result in the page, demonstrating the spec behaviour the feature ships.

interactive.rationale: Real interactive controls drive the page (6 buttons, 0 inputs, 0 selects, 0 ranges, 2 listeners) — well past the static-card bar.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Concept page lede restates and delivers on the framing used in the feature index card.

edge_cases.rationale: Some unsupported / error handling is wired up, but several spec edge cases (boundary inputs, alternate modes, error states) are not surfaced.

references.rationale: Spec is linked but the chromestatus.com/feature/<id> link required by CLAUDE.md rule 3 is missing.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables throughout, no raw hex of note, and button overrides keep background + color paired (CLAUDE.md rules 5 and 6).

openQuestions.title: Demonstrate failure and edge-case branches
openQuestions.detail: Add visible branches for the unsupported / unavailable / error / boundary inputs paths. For built-in AI APIs, that means 'downloadable', 'downloading', 'unavailable' availability states; for HTTP features, error responses and CSP blocks; for CSS features, zero / extreme / inherited values. Today only the happy path is reachable from the UI.

openQuestions.title: Add the chromestatus.com/feature link
openQuestions.detail: CLAUDE.md rule 3 requires every page to embed https://chromestatus.com/feature/4744775089258496. The self-heal slug-fix script reads this link to recover from misslugged folders. Add it under a 'see also' section.

summary: 'WGSL Shader Compilation Tester' delivers the core idea but leaves spec edges, blurb framing, or design-system polish on the table. The open questions list captures the rough edges worth closing in the next pass.
